02 / LOCATE   ▸   MUSCLE ACQUISITION
SIGNAL CHECK   ● ARMED   v1.0
02 / Locate Find your pelvic floor muscle

Before you train it,
find it.

FIG. 02 / MUSCLE LOCATION GUIDE
Kegels for men · The part nobody shows you

Most men have never felt their pelvic floor muscle fire on purpose. Not because it's exotic — because nobody ever pointed at it. You can't train what you can't locate.

Three cues, thirty seconds. No equipment. Your job isn't to work hard yet — it's just to find the signal and recognize the feel before you do a single kegel.

01Stop the stream — the "lift and close" feeling
02Hold back gas — subtle inward lift, not a clench
03Lift, don't crush — elevator up, not a fist
▸ Tip
If your abs, glutes, or thighs take over, back off and go lighter. You're looking for a small signal, not a big effort.
Once you've found it, train it → See the instrument →
03 / INSTRUMENT   ▸   HUD DISPLAY
INPUT STREAM   ● LINKED   v1.0
03 / Instrument The app, live

This is the
instrument.

TRAIN
Session 01 · Day 03
Control Signal
0
25
50
75
MAX
REP 01 / 06 SQUEEZE
Pause Session

The gauge in the hero? That's the app. Hold your phone and it pulses you through each contraction — ramping up, holding steady, easing back — so you train by feel, not by guessing.

No sensors. No clinic. No gym. The bar rises, the haptics rise with it. You match the intensity with your body. Rep count scales as your program advances.

CueHaptic intensity — ramped, held, released
RepsVariable · shaped by your program
SignalPhone vibration synced to the gauge
Session5 to 10 mins, every day
04 / OUTCOMES   ▸   THREE VECTORS
DATA LOG   ● NOMINAL   v1.0
04 / Why it matters Three things change

This is what
changes.

/ 01   Control

Control.

The pelvic floor is the valve on everything below the belt. Train it and "holding on" stops being luck. You decide when things start. You decide when they end.

LongerMore time, on your terms
SteadierLess urgency, fewer surprises
SharperReflex becomes choice
/ 02   Performance

Performance.

This is the part of the core most athletes never train — the one that transfers power between your torso and your legs, and the one that governs sexual function along with it. Strengthen it and you get more output, harder function, faster recovery. A measurable edge where most men never thought to look.

PowerTransfer, not leakage
RecoveryShorter, cleaner
EdgeWhere no one else trains
/ 03   Foundation

Foundation.

The pelvic floor is the floor of your core. Weak floor, weak everything — back pain, bad posture, compromised lifts. Strengthen it and the structure above it stabilizes. This is the muscle everyone assumes their ab work is already hitting. It isn't.

CoreActually engaged
PostureStacked, aligned
LiftsLess lower-back cost

FEED   ● STREAMING   v1.0
05 / From the program Early reports

What it's
doing.

/ Control
"Three weeks in and I'm not second-guessing myself anymore. Didn't expect that to show up this fast."
Dan, 42  ·  Week 3
/ Performance
"My girlfriend noticed before I did. Asked what changed. Couldn't hide the smirk."
Mike R., 36  ·  Day 28
/ Foundation
"Lower back used to complain after deadlift day. Hasn't in a month. This was the gap."
J.C., 48  ·  Week 5

06 / Private by design Your data stays put
Private by design

Your data never
leaves your device.

No account. No sign-up. No health data uploaded anywhere. Everything stays on your phone — completely private by design. Your pelvic floor training is nobody else's business.

● No account ● No sign-up ● No uploads ● On-device only
